behaviors.description: Reject invalid HMAC signature with 401 and do not forward body/event to queue.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure is at module boundary (HTTP handler + signature verifier + forwarding). REAL-INFRA: no real OS/network/subprocess required; in-process HTTP harness is enough. MOCK-HIDE: unit-only test of compare logic could miss accidental forward call. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejected higher level (System) because behavior text is only 'signature does not match... rejected with 401; body is not forwarded' and does not require real network conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Start app with real handler wiring and a spy/fake queue adapter. Send request with valid body but wrong `X-Signature-256`. Assert 401 and assert enqueue/forward method was never called.

behaviors.description: Persist event to queue before returning HTTP 200 acknowledgment.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is ordering/handoff across modules (handler -> persistent queue -> response). Unit is too low. REAL-INFRA: can be verified with local in-process persistence adapter (e.g., sqlite/file-backed queue) without external network/subprocess. MOCK-HIDE: mocking queue write as instant success can hide ordering bugs; use real queue module in test. Rejected System because behavior text only requires sequencing relative to response, not real network/OS failure modes.
behaviors.test_description: Wire handler with real persistent queue module (local test DB/file). Instrument queue `write` completion and response send time. Assert `write` commit/resolve occurs before status 200 is emitted.

behaviors.description: Return HTTP response without waiting for downstream event processing to finish.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure appears in async orchestration between handler, queue, and downstream worker trigger. REAL-INFRA: no real external HTTP/OS primitive is required; controlled delayed processor suffices. MOCK-HIDE: unit test of handler function alone may miss actual wiring where await accidentally blocks response path. Rejected System because behavior text is about waiting semantics, not real network/subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Configure downstream processor to block (e.g., promise resolves after 5s) while enqueue succeeds immediately. Send webhook and assert response returns quickly while processing promise is still pending, then completes later.

behaviors.description: Response must arrive within 3 seconds wall-clock even under concurrent slow downstream load.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: explicit wall-clock SLA under concurrency is sensitive to real HTTP server scheduling/event-loop contention; in-process fakes/timers can hide failures. MOCK-HIDE: mocked transport or fake timers may pass while real socket handling misses 3s bound. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: not applicable. Rejected lower level (Integration) because behavior text explicitly requires 'within 3 seconds wall-clock time' and 'slow and concurrent' conditions.
behaviors.test_description: Run service as real HTTP server, generate background load with slow processing of earlier events, then send fresh webhook(s). Measure end-to-end HTTP latency and assert each response is <=3s while backlog exists.

behaviors.description: Duplicate event ID must not be written to queue twice.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: dedupe correctness is state handoff between handler and persistent queue/id store across requests; unit is too low. REAL-INFRA: local persistent store integration is sufficient; no real network/subprocess needed. MOCK-HIDE: naive mock queue may not enforce uniqueness and can miss duplicate-write bug. Rejected System because behavior text is storage/idempotency logic, not OS/network behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Send same event ID twice through wired handler + real queue persistence layer. Assert first request writes once, second request does not create a new queue record (and verify record count/key uniqueness).

behaviors.description: When queue reports full, return 503 and include Retry-After header.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: this is boundary mapping from queue capacity error state to HTTP contract. Unit-only tests of error mapper can miss wiring issues. REAL-INFRA: no external OS/network dependency beyond local module interaction. MOCK-HIDE: if queue is always mocked as success, full-path behavior is never exercised. Rejected System because behavior text does not require real network failures or subprocess behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Use real handler wiring with queue module configured to signal 'full' (or filled to capacity). Send webhook and assert status 503 plus valid `Retry-After` header; assert no enqueue side effect.

behaviors.description: Persistent queue enforces configured capacity and rejects events beyond limit.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure occurs in persistence/capacity enforcement across queue internals and storage; pure unit can miss real boundary conditions. REAL-INFRA: local DB/filesystem-backed queue is exactly Integration-level infra. MOCK-HIDE: mocked repository will often not reproduce true capacity/transaction behavior. Rejected System because behavior text is local persistent queue capacity enforcement, not external network/subprocess/OS event behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Instantiate queue with small capacity (e.g., 2) against real local persistence backend. Enqueue up to limit successfully, then enqueue one more and assert explicit rejection/error and unchanged persisted count.
